In Dept. 302) is off calendar per request of moving party. Moving party has reported by email that the discovery disputes have been resolved. Judge: Andrew Cheng, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/AC)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-01-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Negele, Esq. (213-612-4400) of Negele & Associates for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant.
Cameron Ward, Esq. (415-543-4111) of Dannis Wolver Kelly for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares the basis for the tentative ruling. Plaintiff argues contract and bond and the 2 parts of the motion. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT PHILADELPHIA INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Plaintiff and cross-defendant Philadelphia Indemnity and Insurance Company's motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied in their entirety. There is a triable dispute whether World Priority Construction and World Priority, LLC are distinct entities. (PIIC's Fact 10 and HACA's response thereto). Among other facts, the two differently titled entities share the same address (Page Depo., Exs. 3 and 5; Ward Dec. Exs. 1 and 4) and the same federal employer identification number. (Page Depo., Exs. 3 and 5; Brewer Depo., 21:24-22:21; 68:10-19; Ex. 12.)

Order is signed in Open Court. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185, email:ADelgado@sftc.org, Reported. (D302)
